title: TRIED Dataset Row
description: A single PyTorch-to-Triton translation example with all attempts, verification results, deterministic failure metadata, and judge decisions for failed attempts. Passing attempts skip the judge.

dataset_id.description: Stable UUID for this exact dataset task. Derived from the full source row: source example_id, op category, PyTorch code, input shapes, input dtypes, rng_seed, and tolerance_policy. Resume uses this field.

example_id.description: Stable source/code UUID from corpus generation. Multiple dataset rows may share this value when the same PyTorch code is exercised with different shapes, seeds, or tolerance policies.

attempts.description: Ordered list of generator-compile-run attempts. Failed attempts include judge labels and advice; passing attempts skip the judge. Index in the array equals attempt_n.

source.description: Immutable inputs that define this example. Copied from the Step 5 training input row (preflight_safe_record.json) when the agent loop starts.

example_id.description: Same source/code UUID as the parent row's example_id.

pytorch_code.description: The PyTorch function or module source the generator is asked to translate. Must be standalone and executable without imports beyond torch.

origin.description: Provenance string. Format: '<source>/<name>'. Source is a repo name (e.g. 'flash-attention', 'mamba', 'torchao', 'tritonbench'), a model namespace ('hf', 'timm'), 'curated', or 'synthetic'. Examples: 'mamba/selective_scan_static', 'tritonbench/layer_norm', 'curated/softmax_fused', 'hf/bert-base-uncased', 'synthetic/fusion'.

input_shapes.description: Shape of each input tensor, as a list of integer lists. Example: [[32, 512, 512]] for a single 3D input.

input_dtypes.description: Dtype of each input tensor. Length must match input_shapes.

rng_seed.description: RNG seed used to generate input tensors. Fixed per example for reproducibility.

tolerance_policy.description: Policy selected during corpus generation/preflight and reused by the dataset verification loop. It is copied from the source row, not recomputed.

attempt.description: One generator-compile-run retry cycle. Failed attempts include judge classification/root-cause/repair-action/advice. Passing attempts set judge fields to null. Training dataset attempts do not include benchmark timings or speedups.

attempt_n.description: Zero-indexed position in the attempts array. Must equal the array index.

prior_advice_applied.description: The judge_fix_suggestion from attempt_n - 1 that this attempt acted on. Null on attempt_n == 0 or if the previous attempt yielded no suggestion.

patch_from_previous.description: Unified diff from the previous attempt's Triton code to this attempt's Triton code. Null on attempt_n == 0.

triton_code.description: The Triton kernel source produced by the generator. Shipped to the verification server as-is.

compile.description: Result of the Triton compilation step on the verification server.

error.description: Captured compiler error text. Null iff status == 'success'.

correctness.description: Numeric agreement between candidate Triton output and reference outputs. Null when compile failed or /run failed before producing correctness stats.

run_error.description: Runtime, launch, timeout, or verification-harness error from /run when compile.status == 'success' but no correctness stats were produced. Null for compile failures and clean numeric pass/fail checks.

failure_symptom.description: Deterministic normalized symptom parsed from compile/run/correctness output. Null when the attempt passed.

judge_classification.description: Judge's classification for failed attempts. Null when the attempt passed.

judge_root_cause.description: Judge-derived semantic root cause for failed attempts. Null when the attempt passed.

judge_repair_action.description: Judge-derived normalized repair action for failed attempts. Null when the attempt passed.

judge_fix_suggestion.description: Natural-language advice from the judge. For non-terminal failed attempts this is consumed by the next retry; for terminal failed attempts it is retained as a label. Null when the attempt passed.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this attempt started (generator call kicked off).

correctness_check.description: Candidate-vs-reference correctness outcome. Training dataset rows record the pass/fail gate and policy only; detailed numeric stats are eval-only.

status.description: 'passed' iff the candidate agreed with both eager and Inductor under tolerance_policy_used.

dataset_outcome.description: Top-level result of the agent loop. compiled_correct = at least one attempt compiled and passed numerics. numeric_fail = at least one attempt produced correctness stats but none passed. runtime_fail = at least one attempt passed /compile but failed during /run before correctness stats. compile_fail = no attempt passed the compile endpoint.

op_category.description: Coarse op family. Closed vocabulary. Finalized after corpus extraction.

judge_classification.description: Judge's classification of an attempt's outcome. Closed vocabulary. The judge is constrained at call time to values in this set.

judge_root_cause.description: Judge-derived semantic root-cause label for a failed attempt. Null on passed attempts.

judge_repair_action.description: Judge-derived normalized repair action for a failed attempt. Null on passed attempts.

failure_symptom.description: Deterministic surface failure symptom parsed from verifier output. This is not a semantic root-cause label.

tolerance_policy.description: Key into packages/shared/src/shared/verification/tolerance.py. Closed vocabulary; that module is the source of truth for atol/rtol values and comparison semantics.
